acceptanceCriteria: Identical normalized failures produce the same deterministic fingerprint across attempts.
Path, timestamp, random ID, and duration noise do not create a new fingerprint when covered by documented normalization rules.

New regressions cannot be hidden by deleting or weakening tests or checks: a failing check that later passes with fewer observed tests, or a disappearing previously-failing test, is flagged as a weakening signal and is not reported as genuine progress.
Infrastructure, timeout, and spawn-error failures are categorized separately from implementation failures and do not by themselves count as an unchanged implementation-failure loop.
Raw command logs and secrets are not persisted: only normalized structured facts (check id, exit code, category, error class, bounded normalized test ids) and digests are stored.
Failure sets are compared across attempts and recorded as first, unchanged, reduced, expanded, or incomparable.
Model-authored diagnosis remains separate from and is explicitly labeled as speculation, distinct from the deterministic Rigor-derived fingerprint and progress facts.
Pure verification and routing stay deterministic for identical inputs; existing v1 attempt and verification artifacts remain parseable (backward compatible).
npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict all pass.
constraints: Do not implement universal semantic log understanding; parse only documented, recognized runner output formats and fall back to an explicit unavailable marker otherwise.

Never auto-classify a failure as flaky; flaky/infrastructure categorization must come from deterministic observation (timeout, spawn error, documented infrastructure patterns) or explicit input, never inferred to make progress look better.
Do not choose an escalation target; that is follow-up issue #14.
Keep model-authored diagnosis (failureClass) separate and explicitly labeled as speculation; deterministic fingerprint and progress fields must be derived by Rigor, not taken from model input.
Do not persist raw logs, stack traces, or secrets; store only normalized facts and digests, bounded in size.
Do not invoke any model from the deterministic CLI; keep pure functions deterministic.
Additive, backward-compatible schema changes only; existing rigor.attempt.v1 and rigor.verification.v1 artifacts must remain valid and parseable.
Do not delete, weaken, skip, or otherwise disable existing tests to pass verification.
Do not modify protected paths, public API compatibility, security boundaries, or unrelated files; stop if such a change becomes necessary.
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
